name: Tết (Vietnamese New Year)
traditions: Family reunions; ancestral altar offerings; lucky money (lì xì) in red envelopes; kumquat and peach blossom displays; bánh chưng rice cakes.
historicalBackground: Tết Nguyên Đán is Vietnam's most important celebration, with roots in Chinese lunar New Year traditions adapted over millennia to Vietnamese culture.
description: Vietnam's most cherished holiday, a celebration of family, ancestors, and renewal during the Lunar New Year.
